text:
{
  "title": "Auxiliary Student Persona Affinity",
  "group_name": "external_student_persona_affinity",
  "family": "external_persona_similarity",
  "summary": "Represent each row by its resemblance to recurring auxiliary-source student personas whose repeated health and lifestyle observations show persistent or mixed health-condition tendencies.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Build source-only personas from student_health_dataset_50k by grouping external rows by student_id, using robust numeric medians, numeric IQR-based variability, categorical modes, categorical mode purity, and health_condition class proportions per student; never join student_id to project id. For each train/test row, compare observed project features to each external persona with a weighted distance: numeric distance is absolute difference from the persona median divided by the global external IQR for that feature and capped at 4 before scaling to [0,1], categorical distance is 0 for mode match, 1 for mismatch, and skipped when the project value is missing; renormalize by observed feature weight and emit an observed-feature fraction. Use the k nearest personas, for example k=25, with inverse-distance or exponential weights to derive weighted auxiliary affinities for unhealthy, at-risk, and fit, the top-vs-second class margin, unhealthy-vs-fit log ratio with epsilon smoothing, weighted persona label entropy, weighted persona categorical purity, weighted numeric variability, nearest-persona distance, and effective support count. If a row has no usable observed features, return global external persona class proportions, maximum entropy, zero observed-feature fraction, and maximum distance. Domain selection is grounded in public health frameworks linking sleep, activity, diet, weight, substance exposure, and physiology to health risk, including AHA Life's Essential 8, CDC sleep-CVH findings, and WHO physical activity guidance: https://www.heart.org/en/healthy-living/healthy-lifestyle/lifes-essential-8 ; https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2020/19_0424.htm ; https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/physical-activity",
  "expected_signal": "The auxiliary file contains repeated observations for the same students, so aggregating to stable personas may expose persistent latent health phenotypes and class tendencies that are less noisy than single-row external matching, especially for separating fit and unhealthy from the dominant at-risk middle class.",
  "risk": "The source class distribution is heavily at-risk, so affinity features may collapse toward the majority unless margins and entropy are retained; the signal can also be unstable if the competition data generation did not preserve external student-level persona structure, and it may partially duplicate broader external similarity groups."
}
